rulings.Case Title: CHARLES LI VS. MEI LING FANG ET AL
rulings.Calendar Matter: MOTION TO QUASH SERVICE OF SUMMONS OR STAY OR DISMISS
rulings.Rulings: Set for Law and Motion/Discovery Calendar on Monday, April 14, 2025 Line 1. DEFENDANT SHUZHEN TU's MOTION TO QUASH SERVICE OF SUMMONS OR STAY OR DISMISS. Continued to April 16, 2025, to be heard at 1:30 p.m. in department 302 before Judge Kahn. For the 9:00 a.m. calendar, all attorneys and parties may appear in Department 302 remotely or in person. Remote hearings will be conducted by videoconference using Zoom. (Dept. 302 Zoom ID 160 409 7690; Passcode 516287.) To appear remotely at the hearing, go to the court's website at sfsuperiorcourt.org under "Online Services," navigate to "Tentative Rulings," and click on the appropriate link, or dial the corresponding phone number. Any party who contests a tentative ruling must send an email to contestdept302tr@sftc.org with a copy to all other parties by 4pm stating, without argument, the portion(s) of the tentative ruling that the party contests. The subject line of the email shall include the line number, case name and case number. The text of the email shall include the name and contact information, including email address, of the attorney or party who will appear at the hearing. Counsel for the prevailing party is required to prepare a proposed order which repeats verbatim the substantive portion of the tentative ruling and must email it to contestdept302tr@sftc.org prior to the hearing even if the tentative ruling is not contested. The court no longer provides a court reporter in the Law & Motion Department. Parties may retain their own reporter, who may appear in the courtroom or remotely. A retained reporter must be a California certified court reporter (CSR), for only a CSR's transcript may be used in California courts. If a CSR is being retained, include in your email all of the following: their name, CSR and telephone numbers, and their individual work email address. =(302/JMQ)

rulings.Case Title: LIANNE GENSLER ET AL VS. BUILDZOOM, INC. ET AL
rulings.Calendar Matter: Notice Of Motion And Motion For Determination Of Good Faith Settlement; Memorandum Of Points And Authorities In Support Thereof
rulings.Rulings: Set for Law and Motion/Discovery Calendar on Monday, April 14, 2025 Line 2. DEFENDANT BUILDZOOM, INC.'s Motion For Determination Of Good Faith Settlement. Defendant Buildzoom, Inc.'s unopposed Motion For Determination Of Good Faith Settlement Pursuant To Code Of Civil Procedure 877.6 (A)(2) is GRANTED IN SUBSTANTIAL PART. Good cause appearing, the court finds the settlement between Plaintiffs Scot Gensler and Lianne Gensler and Defendant Buildzoom, Inc., entered March 7, 2025, is in good faith within the meaning of Code of Civil procedure sections 877 and 876.6. This order shall have the effect provided by Code of Civil procedure sections 877 and otherwise provided by law for an order under sections 877 and 876.6. Any dismissal(s) would flow from the settlement, not this order, and must be separately accomplished by the parties. For the 9:00 a.m. calendar, all attorneys and parties may appear in Department 302 remotely or in person. Remote hearings will be conducted by videoconference using Zoom. (Dept. 302 Zoom ID 160 409 7690; Passcode 516287.) To appear remotely at the hearing, go to the court's website at sfsuperiorcourt.org under "Online Services," navigate to "Tentative Rulings," and click on the appropriate link, or dial the corresponding phone number. Any party who contests a tentative ruling must send an email to contestdept302tr@sftc.org with a copy to all other parties by 4pm stating, without argument, the portion(s) of the tentative ruling that the party contests. The subject line of the email shall include the line number, case name and case number. The text of the email shall include the name and contact information, including email address, of the attorney or party who will appear at the hearing. Counsel for the prevailing party is required to prepare a proposed order which repeats verbatim the substantive portion of the tentative ruling and must email it to contestdept302tr@sftc.org prior to the hearing even if the tentative ruling is not contested. The court no longer provides a court reporter in the Law & Motion Department. Parties may retain their own reporter, who may appear in the courtroom or remotely. A retained reporter must be a California certified court reporter (CSR), for only a CSR's transcript may be used in California courts. If a CSR is being retained, include in your email all of the following: their name, CSR and telephone numbers, and their individual work email address. =(302/JMQ)

rulings.Case Title: SABRINA CHIN VS. TITAN MANAGEMENT GROUP LLC ET AL
rulings.Calendar Matter: Motion And Notice Of Hearing Requesting Dismissal Of Entire Action
rulings.Rulings: Set for Law and Motion/Discovery Calendar on Monday, April 14, 2025 Line 3. PLAINTIFF SABRINA CHIN's Motion And Notice Of Hearing Requesting Dismissal Of Entire Action. Plaintiff Sabrina Chin's unopposed Motion Requesting Dismissal of Entire Action is GRANTED. If the motion were made under Code of Civil Procedure section 581(b)(1) only, the court would likely deny it because dismissal under this statute generally does not require a court order. But here Plaintiff also moves under Labor Code section 2699(s) based on the presence of PAGA claims. (Lab. Code, section 2699(s)(2) ["The superior court shall review and approve any settlement of any civil action filed pursuant to this part."].) Good cause appearing and notice having been provided to LWDA, the court grants Plaintiff's request, approves the settlement agreement and orders dismissal of the entire case, with Plaintiff's individual wage and hour claims dismissed with prejudice, Plaintiff's individual PAGA claims dismissed with prejudice, Plaintiff's representative PAGA claims dismissed without prejudice, and each party bearing their own costs and attorney's fees. Plaintiff shall file Notice(s) of Dismissal on the Judicial Council form consistent with this order and shall present this order when filing the Notice(s). Plaintiff is ordered to submit a proposed order that includes the above text in advance of the time set for the hearing on this motion. For the 9:00 a.m. calendar, all attorneys and parties may appear in Department 302 remotely or in person. Remote hearings will be conducted by videoconference using Zoom. (Dept. 302 Zoom ID 160 409 7690; Passcode 516287.) To appear remotely at the hearing, go to the court's website at sfsuperiorcourt.org under "Online Services," navigate to "Tentative Rulings," and click on the appropriate link, or dial the corresponding phone number. Any party who contests a tentative ruling must send an email to contestdept302tr@sftc.org with a copy to all other parties by 4pm stating, without argument, the portion(s) of the tentative ruling that the party contests. The subject line of the email shall include the line number, case name and case number. The text of the email shall include the name and contact information, including email address, of the attorney or party who will appear at the hearing. Counsel for the prevailing party is required to prepare a proposed order which repeats verbatim the substantive portion of the tentative ruling and must email it to contestdept302tr@sftc.org prior to the hearing even if the tentative ruling is not contested. The court no longer provides a court reporter in the Law & Motion Department. Parties may retain their own reporter, who may appear in the courtroom or remotely. A retained reporter must be a California certified court reporter (CSR), for only a CSR's transcript may be used in California courts. If a CSR is being retained, include in your email all of the following: their name, CSR and telephone numbers, and their individual work email address. =(302/JMQ)
